cases.name: allowed_structural_observation
cases.text: Cassandra records a structural observation over a public artifact and preserves a package hash.

cases.name: allowed_eatf_boundary
cases.text: The EATF receipt verifies package bytes and declared hashes only, not trusted-list legal status.

cases.name: forbidden_legal_validity
cases.text: This is a legally valid trusted list for relying parties.

cases.name: forbidden_signature_validity
cases.text: The trusted-list signature is valid and ready for operational reliance.

cases.name: forbidden_supervisory_status
cases.text: The supervisory authority approved this provider status.

cases.name: forbidden_compliance_alert_absence
cases.text: Compliance passed, public alert issued, and there was no legally relevant change.

caveat: Synthetic wording fixture for local claim-safety scanning only; it is not legal review, publication approval, trusted-list validation, signature validation, supervision, or public alerting.

forbidden_patterns.reason: Cassandra does not determine trusted-list legal effect.
Cassandra does not perform relying-party signature validation.
Cassandra does not supervise or report approvals.
Cassandra does not make compliance judgments.
Dashboard telemetry is not public alerting.
Zero structural diff is not absence of legal change.
